issues.description: Claims oscillation example 'sine of one over x near zero' as a way limits fail to exist. The reference does not mention oscillation as a DNE cause or this specific example.
States the derivative is defined/formalized in 'Module Three'. The reference (Unit 1 only) says nothing about module sequencing or which unit defines derivatives.
issues.fix: verify against later units / remove module-number claim

issues.description: External-resource specifics not in reference: 'OpenStax Calculus Volume One, Chapter Two', 'Khan Academy AP Calc AB Unit One', 'twenty problems', 'AP Classroom Personal Progress Check'. None of these named resources or the count '20' appear in the reference text.
issues.fix: verify resource references externally / not from CED Unit 1

issues.description: Says Module Two covers 'limit laws and the squeeze theorem'. Reference confirms Squeeze Theorem (LIM 1.8) and algebraic properties of limits exist in Unit 1, but does not support the module-sequencing claim that these are 'Module Two'.

notes: Core math claims (limit definition, notation, one-sided limits, three-condition continuity test, removable/jump/infinite discontinuities, instantaneous-vs-average rate) are all consistent with the reference; only specific external resources, problem counts, oscillation example, and module-numbering are absent from the terse Unit 1 reference, all minor.
